title: Enable news pipeline cron
context:
The news ingestor pipeline (`scripts/s012/news_ingestor.py`) has been dark for 28 days — last run Feb 27. The daily briefing is running on stale data.

Diagnosis (by heartbeat builder): pipeline was never broken, it was **never scheduled**. 0 dead letters, 0 errors. Brave API key accessible via keychain. Budget: 900 calls available (0 used in March).

Proposed cron:
```
0 7,19 * * * cd /Users/yu/Desktop/prediction-markets && /opt/homebrew/bin/python3 scripts/s012/news_ingestor.py >> data/news-pipeline.log 2>&1
```

This feeds the daily briefing and improves signal quality for all Oracle predictions.
recommendation: Add the twice-daily cron entry. 900 API calls is ample for 2x/day runs. Fresh news data directly improves prediction quality.

options.label: Twice daily (7am, 7pm)
options.detail: Good coverage, moderate API usage (~60 calls/day)

options.label: Once daily (7am)
options.detail: Conservative, morning briefing only

options.label: Four times daily
options.detail: Better coverage for fast-moving markets, ~120 calls/day

options.label: Skip for now
options.detail: Focus on other Oracle gaps first

title: Whale scanner scheduling
context:
The whale activity scanner (`scripts/whale-activity-scanner.py`, 600 lines) was built overnight. It tracks top Polymarket whales via Polygonscan + Gamma API, scores signals 2-9, outputs JSON.

Currently: script exists but is not scheduled. It can be run standalone or integrated into `oracle-run.py`'s loop.

Dependencies: Polygonscan API (free tier), Gamma API (public). No API keys required for basic scanning.
recommendation: Schedule every 30 min via cron. Whale movements are meaningful but not second-by-second. Integrate output into oracle-run.py signal pipeline.

options.label: Every 15 min (with oracle cron)
options.detail: High frequency, catches fast whale moves

options.label: Every 30 min
options.detail: Good balance of coverage and API courtesy

options.label: Every hour
options.detail: Conservative, sufficient for most whale patterns

options.label: Manual only for now
options.detail: Run on-demand until we validate signal quality

title: Next data source to wire
context:
The Oracle scout identified 6 data sources that are designed but not wired:

| Source | Purpose | Complexity |
|--------|---------|------------|
| FRED | CPI, PCE, unemployment, treasury yields | Low (public API) |
| GDELT | Global events database, 15-min updates | Medium (large data) |
| Kalshi | Fed rate, CPI, NFP prediction markets | Medium (API key needed) |
| Manifold | Long-horizon, AI questions | Low (public API) |
| Guardian | Full-text news for GEO/ECO domains | Low (API key exists) |
| Betfair | Peer-to-peer exchange consensus | High (complex auth) |

Currently wired: Polymarket (Gamma + CLOB), The Odds API, OddsPapi.

5 positions resolve Mar 31. More data sources = better signal quality for the next batch.
recommendation: Wire FRED first — economic data is foundational for ECO domain predictions (Fed rates, CPI) and it's the lowest complexity. Then Guardian for news coverage.

options.label: FRED first (economic fundamentals)
options.detail: Public API, low complexity, high value for ECO domain

options.detail: API key exists, fills the news gap alongside pipeline

options.label: Kalshi first (cross-market signals)
options.detail: Another prediction market = arbitrage opportunities

options.detail: 15-min global events, good for GEO domain

options.label: Hold — focus on existing signals

title: Heartbeat idle behavior
context:
Overnight the heartbeat ran ~52 beats. Of those, only ~12 spawned work. The remaining ~40 were HEARTBEAT_OK idle beats — each consuming an opus coordinator call (~$0.10-0.30 each depending on context length).

At 7-min intervals, 40 idle beats over ~5 hours = roughly $4-12 in token costs with no productive output.

The heartbeat correctly identified there was nothing to do and didn't spawn wasteful work. But the sensing itself has a cost.

Options: adaptive cadence (slow down when idle), night mode, or accept the cost as monitoring overhead.
recommendation: Implement adaptive cadence: after 3 consecutive HEARTBEAT_OK, double the interval (14 min). After 6, double again (28 min). Reset to 7 min on any spawn or signal. This cuts idle costs by ~75% while maintaining responsiveness.

options.label: Adaptive cadence (exponential backoff)
options.detail: 7min -> 14min -> 28min on consecutive idle, reset on activity

options.label: Night mode (longer interval 11pm-7am)
options.detail: 28-min interval during sleeping hours, 7-min during day

options.label: Keep 7-min fixed
options.detail: Accept the cost as monitoring overhead (~$8-24/day)

options.label: Use haiku for idle beats
options.detail: Downgrade to haiku coordinator when no work pending, opus only when spawning

title: Grant cron Full Disk Access for Oracle pipelines
context: macOS TCC (Transparency, Consent, and Control) blocks /usr/sbin/cron from writing to ~/Desktop/ paths. The news pipeline cron entry fires at 7/19 UTC but fails with 'Operation not permitted' because /bin/sh cannot redirect output to data/news-pipeline.log. The whale scanner cron also fails the same way (42 errors in /var/mail/yu). Curiously, oracle-run.py and oracle-deliver.py crons DO work — possibly grandfathered from before a TCC change. The news pipeline has been offline since Feb 27 (29 days). Manual runs work perfectly.
recommendation: Option A (permanent): System Preferences > Privacy & Security > Full Disk Access > add /usr/sbin/cron. Option B (quick workaround): Spawn builder to modify cron entries to avoid shell redirect (use Python subprocess wrapper). Recommend Option A for permanent fix.

options.label: A: Grant cron Full Disk Access (System Preferences)
B: Builder creates Python wrapper to avoid shell redirect

resolution: A: Grant cron Full Disk Access (System Preferences)

title: Run harden.sh on Beta Mac Studio
context: KOS audit shows 3 failures: stealth mode off, hostname leaks identity, Bonjour on. All auto-fixable with sudo.
